name: King's Cross and St Pancras
summary: King's Cross compresses rail hubs, canalside public space, culture, food, and nightlife into a walkable district that judges can understand instantly from a single map view.
signals: Granary Square, Coal Drops Yard, and the canal create a dense loop that feels legible in under 15 minutes of walking.
The station edge makes the area broadly recognizable, which helps the demo land fast without extra explanation.
Quiet cultural stops and louder late-evening venues sit close enough together for graph-based route planning to feel useful.

fallbackWiki.title: King's Cross
fallbackWiki.extract: King's Cross is a central London district shaped by major rail connections and large-scale regeneration around the canal and station frontage.

fallbackWiki.title: St Pancras railway station
fallbackWiki.extract: St Pancras anchors the neighbourhood with long-distance rail links and a station complex that spills directly into the surrounding district.

fallbackWiki.title: British Library
fallbackWiki.extract: The British Library gives the area a strong cultural signal and broadens routes beyond retail and nightlife.

dataSources: Map view: MapLibre GL JS over OpenStreetMap raster tiles.
Route graph: local curated venues with approximate coordinates for stable demos.
Area wiki: live Wikipedia GeoSearch with local fallback summaries when the API is slow.
